frameworks.name: production functions
frameworks.one_line: economic framework decomposing sophistication into multiplicative cognitive inputs

frameworks.name: psychophysics
frameworks.one_line: lawful contextual scaling of capabilities extended to strategic reasoning

frameworks.name: Nash equilibrium / classical game theory
frameworks.one_line: rational-reasoning benchmark that human behavior deviates from

core_question: How do social-reasoning and arithmetic cognitive demands jointly determine strategic sophistication in games?
key_assumptions: strategic sophistication decomposes into separable social and non-social demands
capabilities combine multiplicatively as a production function
psychophysical regularity applies to strategic reasoning
manipulations: social cognitive demand of the game
non-social arithmetic demand of the game
key_findings: social and arithmetic demands act as lawful contextual factors on sophistication
subjects trade off social vs arithmetic capabilities as complexity varies
behavior deviates systematically from Nash equilibrium predictions
interpretations: strategic sophistication can be modeled as a psychophysical product of social and arithmetic capabilities
individual-decision and psychophysics concepts can be hybridized with strategic social reasoning
